info.name: Sales Management System
info.description: API collection for the Sales Management System backend (com.example.demo). Auth and Category are implemented as of Day 2. Other folders are filled in as their APIs are built (see docs.txt PLANNED SCHEDULE).

variable.description: Separate account used only by the Locked Account test, so locking it doesn't interfere with the main Register/Login flow above.
Set automatically by Auth > Login - Success

Category write endpoints are ADMIN-only. There is no public way to create an admin yet (that's Day 4's internal-account API) - promote a registered user with `UPDATE users SET role='ADMIN' WHERE email='...'` in MySQL, then run Auth > Login - Success with that account and copy its token here.
Set automatically by Category > Create Category - Admin
Set automatically by Product > Create Category For Products (setup) - kept separate from categoryId so the Category folder's create/delete lifecycle doesn't interfere with Product tests.
Set automatically by Product > Create Product - Admin (Product 1); also used by the Cart folder.
Set automatically by Product > Create Product To Delete (setup)
Set automatically by Cart > Add To Cart - Success
Second customer's JWT, set by Cart > Login - Second Customer (setup); used only for the cross-user access tests.
Set automatically by Auth > Register - Success; used by Admin/Accounts to lock/unlock that same customer.
Set automatically by Order > Create Product For Order Test (setup)
Set automatically by Order > Create Order 1 - Success; carried through the status-transition happy path to COMPLETED.
Set automatically by Order > Create Order 2 - Success; used for the SHIPPING->CANCELLED note-required test.
Set automatically by Order > Create Low-Stock Product (setup); its stock is forced to 0 via PUT to test order-creation rejection.
Set automatically by Admin/Accounts > Login - Staff (setup)
Set automatically by Admin/Accounts > Create Staff - Admin; used by the Update Staff scenarios (Day 10 / UC-12).

request.description: Separate account dedicated to the Locked Account test below, so locking it doesn't break the main Register/Login flow which reuses {{testEmail}}.
Precondition (no admin-lock API yet, that's Day 4): run `UPDATE users SET status='LOCKED' WHERE email='{{lockedTestEmail}}'` in MySQL after the setup request above and before running this one.

item.name: Update Order Status - Cancel After COMPLETED No Note (expect 400, Day 10 / UC-10)

exec: pm.test('Status is 400 (a reason is required to log the rejected cancel-after-COMPLETED attempt)', () => pm.response.to.have.status(400));

item.name: Update Order Status - Cancel After COMPLETED With Note (expect 409, logged) (Day 10 / UC-10)

exec: pm.test('Status is 409 (COMPLETED is still terminal - the order is NOT cancelled, only logged)', () => pm.response.to.have.status(409));

request.description: Ties back to the Day 2 locked-account scenario - this time the account was locked through the real Admin API instead of manual SQL.

pm.test('Status is 403 (Staff cannot provision accounts)', () => pm.response.to.have.status(403));

request.description: token2's customer never purchased orderProductId, so they haven't earned the right to review it (UC-08).

exec: pm.test('Status is 403 (only reviewable after a COMPLETED order)', () => pm.response.to.have.status(403));

request.description: token's customer owns orderId, which the Order folder took all the way to COMPLETED, so they're eligible to review orderProductId.
